summary: Analyze each Home-tab Word feature: real behavior + concrete implementation plan for the contenteditable clone

specs.feature: Font dialog (Ctrl+D / Home > Font group dialog-box-launcher): a real modal character-formatting dialog with a Font tab and an Advanced tab, plus a live preview, applied to the current selection as a single CSS span.
specs.wordBehavior:
In MS Word, the Font group launcher (or Ctrl+D) opens a tabbed modal "Font" dialog reflecting the formatting of the current selection (or the caret's typing attributes), with a live Preview box at the bottom and a [Set As Default] / [Text EffectsŌĆ”] / OK / Cancel footer.

Font tab:
- Font (family) list with a search/edit box + scrolling list. Picks the typeface.
- Font style: a 4-value list ŌĆö Regular, Italic, Bold, Bold Italic ŌĆö driving font-weight + font-style together.
- Size: edit box + scrolling list of point sizes.
- Font color: dropdown swatch picker (Automatic + theme + standard + More Colors).
- Underline style: dropdown ŌĆö (none), single, words only, double, dotted, dashed, wavy, thick, etc.
- Underline color: swatch picker, enabled only when an underline style is chosen.
- Effects checkboxes: Strikethrough, Double strikethrough, Superscript, Subscript, Small caps, All caps, Hidden (and in real Word also Outline/Shadow/Emboss/Engrave which we omit). Superscript and Subscript are mutually exclusive; Small caps and All caps are mutually exclusive.
- Preview: a sample line ("The quick brown foxŌĆ”" or selected text) rendered with all chosen attributes live.

Advanced tab (Character Spacing):
- Scale: percentage (200%/150%/100%/90%/80%/66%/ŌĆ”) horizontally stretches glyphs.
- Spacing: Normal / Expanded / Condensed + a "By:" point value ŌåÆ adds/removes inter-letter tracking.
- Position: Normal / Raised / Lowered + a "By:" point value ŌåÆ raises/lowers text baseline without changing size.
- (Real Word also has Kerning and an OpenType Ligatures/Number forms block ŌĆö we omit/stub these.)

OK applies every attribute at once to the selection; Cancel discards. "Set As Default" persists as the document default (we scope to a session default for new typing). Hidden text is not shown/printed (toggled by Show/Hide ┬Č in real Word).
specs.uiNeeded:
A modal dialog (WC.dialog, width ~540px) titled "Font" containing a reusable two-tab strip ("Font", "Advanced") built with the existing .tabs/.t/.t.active CSS already in dialogs.css.

FONT TAB body (grid layout):
- Row: "Font:" search input (filters list) + a scrolling list box (div, max-height ~150px, overflow auto) populated from WC.FONTS; "Font style:" list box (Regular/Italic/Bold/Bold Italic); "Size:" number input + scrolling size list from WC.SIZES.
- Row: "Font color:" a swatch button that opens WC.colorPalette in a WC.flyout; "Underline style:" a <select> (none/single/words/double/dotted/dashed/wavy/thick); "Underline color:" swatch button (disabled until a style != none).
- "Effects" fieldset: 7 checkboxes ŌĆö Strikethrough, Double strikethrough, Superscript, Subscript, Small caps, All caps, Hidden (two columns).

ADVANCED TAB body:
- "Scale:" <select> (200/150/100/90/80/66/50%). "Spacing:" <select> (Normal/Expanded/Condensed) + "By:" number (pt). "Position:" <select> (Normal/Raised/Lowered) + "By:" number (pt).

PREVIEW (shared, below the tab bodies): a bordered box (~70px tall, centered) whose inner span's style is recomputed on every control change via a single buildStyle() function ŌĆö same function used on OK.

FOOTER: [Set As Default] (left-aligned, optional), [OK] (primary), [Cancel]. Reuse existing .btn / .btn.primary and WC.dialog footer.
specs.implementationPlan:
Add D.font in dialogs.js and wire it from the launcher + Ctrl+D. Concrete steps:

1) WIRING.
- commands.js: split the launcher from the combo. Keep H.font for the launcher opening the dialog, and make the font-family combo flyout use a separate path. Simplest: add `H.fontDialog = () => WC.Dialogs.font();` and change the launcher control's cmd to "fontDialog" in ribbon-data.js (line ~236), OR detect the launcher in H.font: `H.font = (c, node) => (c && c.id === 'home.font.font-2') ? WC.Dialogs.font() : openFontList(node);` ŌĆö the launcher passes the control object with id 'home.font.font-2', while comboDropdown calls openFontList directly. Use the id check to avoid touching ribbon-data.js.
- app.js line 72: replace the stub with `if (k === 'd') return () => WC.Dialogs.font();`.

2) READ CURRENT STATE (prefill). In D.font, before building, capture E().queryState() and the caret/selection's computed style via getComputedStyle on the selection's anchor element (mirror commands.js currentInlineStyle()). Derive: family (st.computedFontFamily or queryCommandValue fontName), sizePt (st.computedFontSizePt), bold/italic (st.bold/st.italic ŌåÆ style list selection), color (computed color ŌåÆ rgbToHex), underline (st.underline + textDecorationStyle), strike (st.strikethrough), sub/super (st.subscript/st.superscript), small/all caps (fontVariant/textTransform), hidden (display:none ancestor / data-hidden), and advanced (letterSpacing, transform scaleX, verticalAlign/position). Add a small WC-local rgbToHex(rgb) helper in dialogs.js.

3) buildStyle() ŌåÆ returns a style object/string from all controls:
- fontFamily: chosen family.
- fontSize: size + 'pt'.
- fontWeight: 'bold' if style is Bold/Bold Italic else 'normal'.
- fontStyle: 'italic' if Italic/Bold Italic else 'normal'.
- color: chosen hex (omit if Automatic).
- textDecorationLine: join of ['underline' if underline style!=none] + ['line-through' if strikethrough or doubleStrike]; textDecorationStyle: 'double' when Double strikethrough OR when underline style is double; for distinct under+strike use textDecoration shorthand. textDecorationColor: underline color when set.
- Effects: superscriptŌåÆverticalAlign:super;fontSize:smaller (or use exec('superscript')); subscript similarly; smallCapsŌåÆfontVariant:'small-caps'; allCapsŌåÆtextTransform:'uppercase'; hiddenŌåÆadd class 'hidden-text' (CSS: .hidden-text{display:none} when not in Show/Hide mode) and data-hidden="1".
- Advanced: letterSpacing from Expanded(+By pt)/Condensed(-By pt); transform/display:inline-block + transform:scaleX(scale/100) for Scale (note: inline transforms need inline-block, so wrap with display:inline-block when scale!=100); verticalAlign offset via position:relative; top:(Lowered +By / Raised -By)pt for Position.

4) PREVIEW: keep one preview span; an update() handler attached (change/input/click) to every control calls Object.assign(previewSpan.style, buildStyle()) and toggles caps classes; preview text = selected text (sel.toString()) or 'AaBbCc ŌĆö The quick brown fox'.

5) APPLY (OK): focus editor, restore E().savedRange (capture it at dialog open since opening the modal blurs the editor ŌĆö store `const saved = E().savedRange` and restore before applying). Then apply as ONE span to avoid nested-span churn:
   - If selection is non-collapsed: build a span, set span.style via buildStyle(), surroundContents/extractContents like applyInlineStyle does, set caps classes + data-hidden. Implement a local applyFontSpan(styleObj, classes) in dialogs.js (modeled on Editor.applyInlineStyle but applying a whole style object) ŌĆö or extend editor.js with Editor.applyInlineStyles(obj) that does the same wrap with Object.assign(span.style,obj). Recommended: add Editor.applyInlineStyles(styleObj, classList) to editor.js (one new method) so the wrap logic stays in one place; the Font dialog calls it once.
   - If collapsed caret: same zero-width-span path as applyInlineStyle so subsequent typing inherits, and remember as session default.
   - For super/subscript specifically, prefer E().exec('superscript')/('subscript') first (so it toggles cleanly and queryState reflects it), then apply remaining props via the span.
   - After apply: E().dirty=true; E().repaginate(); E().updateStatus(); E().emit(); WC.Ribbon.setComboValue('font',family); WC.Ribbon.setComboValue('fontSize',String(size)); WC.Ribbon.setColorBar('fontColor',color).

6) Color pickers: reuse WC.colorPalette inside WC.flyout anchored to the swatch button; onPick sets the swatch background + a local var and calls update(). Use opts {automatic:true,autoLabel:'Automatic'} for font color and {noColor:true} for underline color.

7) CSS: add to dialogs.css a small block: a .font-dlg grid, .font-list/.size-list (border, max-height:150px, overflow:auto, .opt.sel highlight), .fx-grid (2-col checkbox grid), .preview-box (border, height:70px, display:flex, center). Add to editor.css: .hidden-text{display:none} and .show-marks .hidden-text{display:inline;color:#999;text-decoration:underline dotted} so Show/Hide reveals hidden text (matches H.showHide which toggles .show-marks on #editor).

This is a full, working subset: family/style/size/color/underline-style+color/all 7 effects/scale/spacing/position + live preview, applied atomically to the selection.
specs.testPlan:
Add cases to the in-renderer test battery (the WORD_CLONE_READY harness):
1) Open: call WC.Dialogs.font(); assert a .dialog with title "Font" and a .tabs strip with two .t tabs exists; assert preview-box present.
2) Tab switch: click the Advanced .t; assert Advanced controls (Scale/Spacing/Position) visible and Font-tab list hidden; click back.
3) Prefill: select a run that is bold+italic 14pt red (insert known HTML, select it, open dialog); assert style list = 'Bold Italic', size input = '14', color swatch background Ōēł #FF0000, family matches.
4) Apply basic: select plain text, open dialog, set family=Georgia, size=18, style=Bold, color=#0070C0, underline=double; OK; assert the selection is now wrapped in a single span whose computed fontFamily contains Georgia, fontSizeŌēł24px (18pt), fontWeightŌēź600, textDecorationLine includes underline, textDecorationStyle='double', color rgb(0,112,192). Assert NOT nested 5-deep (querySelectorAll('span span span span span') length 0 within the applied run).
5) Effects: check Small caps ŌåÆ assert span has class/fontVariant small-caps; check All caps ŌåÆ assert it cleared Small caps (mutual exclusion) and textTransform uppercase; check Superscript then Subscript ŌåÆ assert mutual exclusion and queryState().subscript true.
6) Hidden: check Hidden, OK; assert run has .hidden-text and is display:none; toggle WC.Commands H.showHide (#editor.show-marks) ŌåÆ assert it becomes visible.
7) Advanced: set Spacing=Expanded By 2pt, Scale=80%, Position=Raised By 3pt; OK; assert span letterSpacingŌēł2pt, an inline-block scaleX(0.8) transform present, and a positive top offset (raised).
8) Live preview: programmatically toggle Bold checkbox/style and assert previewSpan computed fontWeight updates WITHOUT calling OK (no document mutation: editor.getHTML() unchanged until OK).
9) Ctrl+D: dispatch a ctrl+d keydown; assert the Font dialog opens (app.js wiring).
10) Cancel: open, change controls, Cancel; assert editor.getHTML() unchanged.

specs.wordBehavior:
In real MS Word this is the "A" with a glowing blue outline button in the Font group. Clicking the dropdown arrow opens a menu:
1) TOP GALLERY (15 preset WordArt-style text effects in a 5x3 grid) ŌĆö each applies a bundled combination of fill color, outline, and shadow at once.
2) OUTLINE submenu (cascades): a color palette (Theme Colors / Standard Colors / No Outline / More Outline Colors) plus "Weight" (a sub-list of stroke weights: ┬╝pt, ┬Įpt, ┬Špt, 1pt, 1┬Įpt, 2┬╝pt, 3pt, 4┬Įpt, 6pt, More Lines) and "Dashes" (solid/dash patterns ŌĆö visual only on glyph outline).
3) SHADOW submenu (cascades): "No Shadow"; an Outer group (9 directional presets: offsets in 8 compass directions + center), Inner group (9 presets), Perspective group (Top-Left/Top-Right/etc.), and "Shadow OptionsŌĆ”" (a Format Text Effects task pane with Color, Transparency, Size, Blur, Angle, Distance sliders).
4) REFLECTION submenu: "No Reflection" + a Reflection Variations grid (Tight/Half/Full reflection at 4pt/8pt offset ŌĆö 9 presets) + "Reflection OptionsŌĆ”".
5) GLOW submenu: "No Glow" + Glow Variations grid (5 sizes x theme accent colors), a "Glow Color" palette, and "Glow OptionsŌĆ”".
6) NUMBER STYLES submenu (OpenType numeral forms, applied via font-feature-settings/font-variant-numeric): Default, Proportional Lining, Tabular Lining, Proportional Oldstyle, Tabular Oldstyle. (Sub-options: lining vs oldstyle figure shapes; proportional vs tabular spacing.)
7) LIGATURES submenu: None, Standard Only, Standard and Contextual, Historical and Discretionary, All.
8) STYLISTIC SETS submenu: a gallery of "Individual" alternate glyph sets (Set 1ŌĆ”Set 20 in Word) plus "Default", applied via font-feature-settings 'ss01'..'ss20'. Word previews each set live in the menu.
All effects are character-level (apply to the selected run) and combine; e.g. you can have outline + shadow + glow simultaneously, and the preset gallery applies several at once. Setting a "No X" / "Default" sub-item removes only that effect's CSS, leaving the others.
specs.uiNeeded:
A dropdown (WC.flyout) anchored on the Text Effects button. Because WC.flyout only keeps ONE flyout open at a time (closeFly() runs on each open), do NOT attempt true OS-style cascading submenus. Instead use the established two-level pattern: the top-level flyout has section header + items; clicking a category item (Outline/Shadow/Reflection/Glow/Number Styles/Ligatures/Stylistic Sets) opens a SECOND flyout anchored on that same row (re-using WC.flyout(rowNode, builder)), exactly like the existing colorMenu/underlineMenu do. Top-level flyout contents, in order:
- fly-header "Text Effects"
- A preset gallery: a grid (el div, gridTemplateColumns repeat(5,1fr)) of 15 cells, each cell an "A" styled with that preset's combined CSS (fill/outline/shadow), onClick applies the bundle.
- fly-sep
- flyItem "Outline" (icon optional) -> opens outline sub-flyout (color palette + Weight list + No Outline)
- flyItem "Shadow" -> sub-flyout: "No Shadow" + Outer(9) + Inner(9) + Perspective(4) preset grid + "Shadow OptionsŌĆ”"
- flyItem "Reflection" -> sub-flyout: "No Reflection" + 9 variation cells + "Reflection OptionsŌĆ”"
- flyItem "Glow" -> sub-flyout: "No Glow" + 5 size variations x accent colors grid + "Glow Color" (colorPalette) + "Glow OptionsŌĆ”"
- fly-sep
- flyItem "Number Styles" -> sub-flyout listing the 5 numeral modes (use flyItem; show a check on the active one)
- flyItem "Ligatures" -> sub-flyout listing the 5 ligature modes
- flyItem "Stylistic Sets" -> sub-flyout with "Default" + a grid of "Set 1".."Set 20" cells, each previewing the word "ffi 0123" with that ss0n applied
"Shadow/Reflection/Glow OptionsŌĆ”" open WC.dialog modals (Format Text Effects) with range sliders that live-apply to the selection. The preset grids reuse the .color-grid / cell visual idiom already in CSS; add a small .te-cell class.

specs.implementationPlan:
FILE A ŌĆö /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/js/editor.js: add a multi-property helper next to applyInlineStyle (so effects that need >1 CSS prop, e.g. outline = webkitTextStroke + webkitTextFillColor, or glow = several stacked text-shadows, apply atomically):
  applyInlineStyles(styles) { this.node.focus(); this.restoreRange(); const sel=getSelection(); if(!sel.rangeCount) return; const range=sel.getRangeAt(0); const span=el('span'); for(const k in styles){ if(styles[k]==null||styles[k]==='') span.style.removeProperty(k.replace(/[A-Z]/g,m=>'-'+m.toLowerCase())); else span.style[k]=styles[k]; } if(range.collapsed){ span.appendChild(document.createTextNode('ŌĆŗ')); range.insertNode(span); const r=document.createRange(); r.setStart(span.firstChild,1); r.collapse(true); sel.removeAllRanges(); sel.addRange(r);} else { try{range.surroundContents(span);}catch(e){span.appendChild(range.extractContents()); range.insertNode(span);} sel.removeAllRanges(); const r=document.createRange(); r.selectNodeContents(span); sel.addRange(r);} this.saveRange(); this.dirty=true; this.repaginate(); this.updateStatus(); this.emit(); }
  Also add removeInlineStyle(prop) for the "No X / Default" items: walk the selection's spans and clear that one style prop (or set to '' / 'normal'). Simplest robust approach mirroring clearAllFormatting: applyInlineStyles({ [prop]: '' }) wrapping the run sets the property to empty on a fresh span which overrides the inherited effect ŌĆö acceptable; for a cleaner removal, query span ancestors of the selection and delete style[prop]. Spec the simple override path first.

FILE B ŌĆö /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/js/commands.js: 
1) Add handler: H.textEffectsAndTypography = (c, node) => textEffectsMenu(node);
2) In Commands.dropdown add: if (cmd === 'textEffectsAndTypography') return textEffectsMenu(node);  (place near the other custom-dropdown branches so the split/dropdown generic path is bypassed.)
3) Implement the menu + sub-builders. Concrete CSS mappings (camelCase DOM keys; values are CSS strings):
   OUTLINE: applyInlineStyles({ webkitTextStroke: weight+' '+color, webkitTextFillColor: 'currentColor' }). Default weight '1px'. Word weights map: ┬╝ptŌåÆ0.33px, ┬ĮptŌåÆ0.67px, ┬ŠptŌåÆ1px, 1ptŌåÆ1.33px, 1┬ĮptŌåÆ2px, 2┬╝ptŌåÆ3px, 3ptŌåÆ4px, 4┬ĮptŌåÆ6px, 6ptŌåÆ8px (px = pt*1.3333). "No Outline" ŌåÆ applyInlineStyles({ webkitTextStroke:'', webkitTextFillColor:'' }). Reuse WC.colorPalette for the color pick (like colorMenu) then re-apply stroke with chosen color.
   SHADOW: each preset is a textShadow string. Outer presets (offset 2px, blur 2px, color rgba(0,0,0,.45)) in 8 directions: e.g. Bottom-Right '2px 2px 2px rgba(0,0,0,.45)', Bottom '0 2px 2px ...', Bottom-Left '-2px 2px 2px ...', Right '2px 0 2px ...', Center '0 0 3px ...', Left '-2px 0 2px ...', Top-Right '2px -2px 2px ...', Top '0 -2px 2px ...', Top-Left '-2px -2px 2px ...'. Inner presets simulated with a tighter darker shadow (Chromium has no real inner text-shadow, approximate with '0 0 1px rgba(0,0,0,.6) inset'-unsupported ŌåÆ use small dark offset). Perspective presets use larger blur + skew via transform on the span (transform:'perspective(...)' is for blocks; for inline use a stronger lower shadow '0 6px 6px rgba(0,0,0,.35)'). Apply via applyInlineStyles({textShadow: preset}). "No Shadow" ŌåÆ textShadow:''.
   REFLECTION: prefer native applyInlineStyles({ webkitBoxReflect: 'below 4px linear-gradient(transparent 40%, rgba(255,255,255,.4) 100%)' }) ŌĆö works because we ship one Chromium engine. Variations change the offset (4px/8px) and gradient stop (Tight 60%, Half 50%, Full 35%) and opacity. "No Reflection" ŌåÆ webkitBoxReflect:''.
   GLOW: stack multiple same-color blurred text-shadows: const glow=(color,r)=> Array(3).fill('0 0 '+r+'px '+color).join(', '); apply applyInlineStyles({textShadow: glow(color,r)}). Sizes r = 4/6/8/10/12px = Word's 5pt/8pt/11pt/14pt/18pt variations. Accent colors from the existing THEME/STANDARD palette. NOTE: glow and drop-shadow both use textShadow ŌĆö if both wanted, compose by reading current span textShadow and appending; spec: glow replaces textShadow (matches Word treating them in same property is acceptable for the clone, document the limitation). "No Glow" ŌåÆ textShadow:''. "Glow Color" reuses WC.colorPalette.
   NUMBER STYLES (font-feature-settings + font-variant-numeric): Default ŌåÆ fontVariantNumeric:'normal', fontFeatureSettings:'normal'; Proportional Lining ŌåÆ fontVariantNumeric:'lining-nums proportional-nums' (feature 'lnum' 1,'pnum' 1); Tabular Lining ŌåÆ 'lining-nums tabular-nums' ('lnum','tnum'); Proportional Oldstyle ŌåÆ 'oldstyle-nums proportional-nums' ('onum','pnum'); Tabular Oldstyle ŌåÆ 'oldstyle-nums tabular-nums' ('onum','tnum'). Use fontVariantNumeric (cleaner) as primary, fontFeatureSettings as fallback string.
   LIGATURES (font-variant-ligatures): None ŌåÆ 'none'; Standard Only ŌåÆ 'common-ligatures no-discretionary-ligatures no-historical-ligatures no-contextual'; Standard and Contextual ŌåÆ 'common-ligatures contextual'; Historical and Discretionary ŌåÆ 'discretionary-ligatures historical-ligatures'; All ŌåÆ 'common-ligatures discretionary-ligatures historical-ligatures contextual'. Apply via applyInlineStyles({fontVariantLigatures: value}).
   STYLISTIC SETS (font-feature-settings): Default ŌåÆ fontFeatureSettings:'normal'; Set n ŌåÆ fontFeatureSettings:'"ss'+String(n).padStart(2,'0')+'" 1'. Build a grid of cells previewing e.g. 'A a g 1' with the candidate ss applied inline so the user sees the alternate (effect only visible with fonts that ship the set, e.g. Calibri/Aptos/Gabriola ŌĆö document that on fonts without the set it is a no-op, exactly as Word behaves).
4) Sub-flyout helper pattern (mirrors colorMenu): each category opens WC.flyout(rowNode, builder). For grids reuse el('div',{style:{display:'grid',...}}) and small cells like bulletMenu does; each cell onClick: WC.closeFlyouts(); applyEffect(...). Add a 'No ŌĆ”' / 'Default' row at top using WC.flyItem.

specs.testPlan:
Add cases to the in-renderer test battery (the same harness that exercises WC.Commands). For each, programmatically select a known run of text in #editor (place a range over a span/paragraph via document.createRange + selection.addRange, set WC.Editor.savedRange), invoke the handler/sub-action directly, then assert the resulting span's inline style:
1) Outline: call the outline-apply path with weight ┬Špt + color #C00000 ŌåÆ assert the new span getAttribute('style') contains 'text-stroke' (or el.style.webkitTextStroke === '1px rgb(192, 0, 0)') and webkitTextFillColor set. Then "No Outline" ŌåÆ assert webkitTextStroke cleared.
2) Shadow: apply Bottom-Right preset ŌåÆ assert span.style.textShadow includes '2px 2px'. No Shadow ŌåÆ textShadow==='' .
3) Reflection: apply Full ŌåÆ assert span.style.webkitBoxReflect startsWith('below') and contains 'linear-gradient'. No Reflection clears it.
4) Glow: apply size 8px accent #4472C4 ŌåÆ assert span.style.textShadow matches /0px 0px 8px/ and the color appears 3 times (stacked). No Glow clears.
5) Number Styles: apply Tabular Oldstyle ŌåÆ assert span.style.fontVariantNumeric.includes('oldstyle-nums') && includes('tabular-nums'). Default ŌåÆ 'normal'.
6) Ligatures: apply 'None' ŌåÆ fontVariantLigatures==='none'; 'All' ŌåÆ contains 'discretionary-ligatures'.
7) Stylistic Sets: apply Set 4 ŌåÆ assert span.style.fontFeatureSettings === '"ss04" 1'; Default ŌåÆ 'normal'.

specs.feature: Clipboard task pane (Clipboard group dialog-box-launcher) ŌĆö the Office Clipboard collecting up to 24 cut/copied items, with Paste All, Clear All, and click-an-item-to-paste.
specs.wordBehavior:
In real MS Word, clicking the tiny dialog-box-launcher arrow at the bottom-right of the Home > Clipboard group opens the Office Clipboard task pane (docks left by default, can dock right/float). Behavior:
- It silently collects every Cut and Copy you make (in Word and across Office apps) up to a maximum of 24 items. The 25th copy pushes out the oldest (FIFO).
- Each entry shows an icon indicating source/type plus a preview: ~50 chars of text for text items, a thumbnail for pictures. Newest item is at the top.
- Single-click an item pastes it at the current insertion point, preserving its formatting. Hovering an item reveals a dropdown chevron with Paste and Delete.
- "Paste All" button pastes every collected item, in collection order (oldest first), into the document at the caret.
- "Clear All" empties the whole clipboard collection (the list becomes empty with the placeholder text "Clipboard empty. Copy or cut to collect items.").
- An "Options" button at the bottom holds toggles: "Show Office Clipboard Automatically", "Show Office Clipboard When Ctrl+C Pressed Twice", "Collect Without Showing Office Clipboard", "Show Office Clipboard Icon on Taskbar", "Show Status Near Taskbar When Copying". A header shows a counter like "12 of 24 - Clipboard".
Sub-options to model: per-item Paste / Delete (hover chevron menu), Paste All, Clear All, the item count header, the empty-state placeholder, and (as approximated menu entries) the Options toggles.
specs.uiNeeded: A left-docked task pane (reuse the existing .taskpane component, NOT .taskpane.right, since Office Clipboard docks left). Exact contents, top to bottom: 1. .tp-head: title "Clipboard" + close X (WC.icon('win_close', 12)). 2. A toolbar row (.cb-actions) with two buttons: "Paste All" and "Clear All" (both use existing .btn class; disabled/greyed when the collection is empty). 3. A counter line (.cb-count) reading "N of 24 - Clipboard". 4. .tp-body containing the scrollable item list (.cb-list). Each item is a .cb-item row: a small type icon (paste/clipboard glyph) on the left, a text/thumbnail preview (max ~50 chars, ellipsised; images render a small thumbnail), and a hover-revealed chevron (.cb-item-menu) that opens a WC.flyout with "Paste" and "Delete (X)" entries. Clicking the item body pastes it. 5. Empty state: when the list is empty, .cb-list shows a centered placeholder div "Clipboard empty. Copy or cut to collect items." and the Paste All / Clear All buttons are disabled. 6. Bottom .cb-options: a single "Options Ō¢Š" WC.flyItem-style button opening a flyout of the five Office-Clipboard toggle labels (checkbox-styled flyItems; the toggles drive real local behavior where applicable, e.g. "Show Office Clipboard Automatically" auto-opens the pane on copy). No modal WC.dialog is needed ŌĆö this is a task pane, matching D.findPane / D.navPane / D.stylesPane patterns.

specs.feature: Format Painter (Home Ō¢Ė Clipboard) ŌĆö double-click sticky/locked mode, cursor feedback, and the exact set of inline styles copied. The single-click "copy once, apply once" path already exists in commands.js (H.formatPainter ŌåÆ currentInlineStyle + armPainter).
specs.wordBehavior: In real MS Word: a single click on Format Painter copies formatting from the current selection/caret and applies it to the next selection, then turns off (one-shot). A double-click locks the tool on, applying the copied formatting to every subsequent selection until the user presses Esc, clicks the button again, or re-presses Ctrl+Shift+C. The paintbrush cursor (I-beam + brush) shows while active. It copies character formatting (font, size, bold/italic/underline incl. style+color, strikethrough, sub/superscript, font color, highlight, small caps, spacing, language) and, when the source includes a paragraph mark/whole paragraph, paragraph formatting (alignment, indents, spacing, list, style). It never copies the text itself. Ctrl+Shift+C copies formatting; Ctrl+Shift+V applies it.
specs.uiNeeded:
No dropdown, dialog, or task pane. UI surface is entirely on the existing single ribbon button (ribbon-data.js id "home.clipboard.format-painter", cmd "formatPainter", currently type "button"):

- Cursor feedback over #editor: a paintbrush-with-I-beam cursor. Implement via a body/#editor class ".fp-active" that sets cursor to an inline SVG data-URI paintbrush (cursor: url("data:image/svg+xml,...") 4 2, text;). Add a tiny visual hint: keep WC.toast guidance on arm (single: "select text to apply once"; double: "locked ŌĆö select text repeatedly, Esc to stop").
- Optional status hint: none required; the toast plus persistent toggled button is the Word-faithful affordance.

specs.feature: Styles task pane + Apply Styles + Create a Style (Styles group dialog-box-launcher and gallery extras)
specs.wordBehavior:
In MS Word the Styles group has three style entry points that all draw from one shared style registry (built-in named styles plus user-created custom styles):

1. Styles gallery (Home > Styles): a horizontal scrolling gallery of "Quick Styles" with live previews (each cell rendered in the style it applies). Hover shows a live-preview of the style on the current paragraph/selection; click applies it. Paragraph styles apply to the whole paragraph(s) the selection touches; character/linked styles apply to the selection run. Below the gallery scroll arrows is a "More" (down-chevron) button that drops the full gallery plus three commands at the bottom: "Create a Style", "Clear Formatting", and "Apply Styles..." (Ctrl+Shift+S).
   - Create a Style: opens "Create New Style from Formatting" ŌĆö a small dialog with a Name field and a live preview of the current selection's formatting; OK adds a new Quick Style built from the formatting at the caret. A "Modify..." button expands to the full style dialog (paragraph/character type, based-on, font, etc.).
   - Clear Formatting: removes all character and paragraph formatting, resetting the selection to the Normal style (same as Home > Font > Clear All Formatting / Ctrl+Spacebar + Ctrl+Q).
   - Apply Styles (Ctrl+Shift+S): a tiny floating "Apply Styles" box with a style-name combo (type or pick a style name), Reapply/Modify buttons. Applies the typed style.

2. Styles dialog-box launcher (small arrow at the group's corner, Ctrl+Alt+Shift+S): opens the Styles task pane docked on the right. It lists EVERY available style (built-in: Normal, No Spacing, Heading 1-9, Title, Subtitle, Quote, Intense Quote, Strong, Emphasis, Subtle/Intense Emphasis, List Paragraph, etc.) plus any custom styles, each row rendered in its own style (paragraph styles show a paragraph-mark glyph, character styles an 'a' glyph, linked styles both). The currently-active style is highlighted. Clicking a row applies it. Each row has a hover dropdown (Update to Match Selection, Modify, Select All Instances, Rename, Delete). There is a "Show Preview" checkbox (renders rows in their styles), an "Options..." link (which styles to show: recommended / in use / in current document / all), and three buttons at the bottom: New Style (= Create a Style), Style Inspector, Manage Styles.

All three surfaces share state: a custom style created via "Create a Style" immediately appears in the gallery, the pane, and the Apply Styles box, and is highlighted when the caret sits in text using it.
specs.uiNeeded:

Each row carries a hover "ŌĆ”"/chevron that opens a WC.flyout with: Update {name} to Match Selection, ModifyŌĆ”, Select All N Instances, RenameŌĆ” (custom only), Delete (custom only). Click on the row body applies the style.

C) Apply Styles floating box (Ctrl+Shift+S) ŌĆö small WC.dialog or compact flyout near the selection:

D) Gallery More-menu (in renderStylesGallery, the existing `all` button): currently calls stylesPane(); change it to a WC.flyout listing all style cells PLUS a separator and the three commands "Create a Style", "Clear Formatting", "Apply StylesŌĆ”". The inline gallery should also include any custom styles.
specs.implementationPlan:
Central registry + three surfaces. All code is real (CSS spans, no execCommand needed for custom styles ŌĆö they apply a class whose CSS we inject).

1) STYLE REGISTRY (new, in formatting.js). Define a single source of truth:
   - `WC.Styles.BUILTIN` = ordered array of {name, type:'paragraph'|'character'|'linked', css (preview string), apply(blocks/range)}. Reuse the exact behaviors already in WC.applyNamedStyle (Normal, No Spacing, Heading 1-4, Title, Subtitle, Quote, Intense Quote, Strong, Emphasis, Subtle/Intense Emphasis, List Paragraph) ŌĆö refactor that switch into per-style entries so the pane, gallery, and Apply box all read the same list. Add Heading 5/6 for completeness.
   - `WC.Styles.custom = []` for user styles: each {name, type, className (e.g. 'wcs-style1'), declarations:{prop:val,...}}.
   - `WC.Styles.all()` returns BUILTIN names + custom names. `WC.Styles.find(name)`.
   - A `<style id="wc-custom-styles">` element appended to <head> on init; `WC.Styles.injectCSS()` rewrites it from `custom` (selectors `.wcs-xxx{...}` for character styles and `p.wcs-xxx,h1.wcs-xxx,...{...}` / generic `.wcs-xxx` for paragraph styles). This makes styles real and reusable, and survives getHTML/setHTML because the class names persist in the document (round-trip note below).

2) CAPTURE CURRENT FORMATTING (new helper `WC.Styles.captureSelection()`):
   - Reuse the existing pattern from commands.js `currentInlineStyle()` (getComputedStyle of the anchor node's element). Collect fontFamily, fontSize (convert pxŌåÆpt via the /1.3333 factor already used in editor.queryState computedFontSizePt), fontWeight, fontStyle, textDecorationLine, color, and (for paragraph type) textAlign, lineHeight, margin, backgroundColor of the block from E().selectedBlocks()[0].
   - Return a declarations object {} of only the non-default props (skip values equal to the Normal baseline: Calibri/11pt/400/normal/none/rgb(0,0,0)) so the custom class is minimal and meaningful.

3) APPLY A STYLE (`WC.applyNamedStyle(name)` ŌĆö extend existing):
   - If name is a BUILTIN, run its existing apply() (the refactored switch). Keep the existing active-cell reflection line.
   - If name is a custom style: paragraph/linked ŌåÆ E().selectedBlocks().forEach(b => { strip other wcs-* classes; b.classList.add(className) }); character ŌåÆ wrap selection in a span.className=className (use the same surroundContents/extractContents fallback already used by applyInlineStyle and Comments.add). Then E().dirty=true; E().repaginate(); E().updateStatus(); E().emit().
   - After applying, refresh pane highlight + gallery active cell via a new `WC.Styles.refreshActive()` driven off E().onStateChange / queryState.block + caret class detection.

4) STYLES TASK PANE (rewrite D.stylesPane in dialogs.js):
   - Build from `WC.Styles.all()`. Each `.sl-item`: leading `.sl-glyph` (┬Č / a / ┬Ča), `.sl-name` (text). When Show-Preview checkbox on, add class `preview` and set inline `style` from the style's css preview string (same map the gallery uses, plus custom declarations). Click body ŌåÆ WC.applyNamedStyle(name) and close-or-keep open (Word keeps it open).
   - Per-row dropdown: a small chevron that opens WC.flyout(rowChevron, fly => WC.flyItem('Update {name} to Match Selection',ŌĆ”), 'ModifyŌĆ”' ŌåÆ openCreateStyleDialog(prefillName), 'Select All N Instances' ŌåÆ select all nodes carrying that style/tag via a Range over document, 'RenameŌĆ”'/'Delete' for custom ŌåÆ mutate registry + injectCSS + reflow document classnames + re-render pane).
   - "Update to Match Selection": capture current formatting into the existing style's declarations (for custom) or toast for built-ins (Word allows it; minimal = custom only), injectCSS, repaginate.
   - Footer buttons + Options select as described. Re-render the list after any registry mutation via a `renderList()` closure.

5) CREATE-A-STYLE DIALOG (new D.createStyle in dialogs.js):
   - On open: const decl = WC.Styles.captureSelection(); name input default = WC.Styles.nextName() (Style1, Style2ŌĆ”). Type <select>. Live preview <div> styled from decl + selected text (or sample sentence).
   - OK: validate non-empty unique name (toast + keep open if dup, mirroring insertTable's `return false` keep-open pattern). Create className = 'wcs-' + slug(name) + uniquifier; push {name,type,className,declarations:decl} to WC.Styles.custom; WC.Styles.injectCSS(); WC.applyNamedStyle(name) to apply to current selection; refresh gallery (re-render the styles gallery cell list ŌĆö expose `WC.Ribbon.refreshStylesGallery()` that rebuilds the grid, or append a cell directly); WC.toast('Style "{name}" created.'); close.
   - ModifyŌĆ”: optional ŌĆö toast 'Full Modify Style dialog is approximated' (document-only) OR reuse the same dialog with extra font/size/bold/italic/color controls (feasible, see subOptions).

6) CLEAR FORMATTING command (wire the gallery/More entry and a dedicated handler):
   - Reuse the already-working `H.clearAllFormatting` in commands.js (does removeFormat + strip block style + formatBlock('p')). Add the missing step: also strip custom wcs-* and doc-title/doc-subtitle classes from selectedBlocks so it truly returns to Normal. Map gallery "Clear Formatting" entry and pane usage to it.

7) GALLERY WIRING (ribbon.js renderStylesGallery):
   - Include custom styles in the rendered cells (append after built-ins; preview from declarations).
   - Change the More (`all`) button from `WC.Dialogs.stylesPane()` to a WC.flyout containing all style cells + WC.flySep() + flyItems 'Create a Style' (ŌåÆ D.createStyle), 'Clear Formatting' (ŌåÆ H.clearAllFormatting), 'Apply StylesŌĆ”' (ŌåÆ D.applyStyles). Keep a separate path so the dialog-box launcher (already wired: commands.js launcher map `styles: () => WC.Dialogs.stylesPane()`) opens the full pane.
   - Add `WC.Ribbon.refreshStylesGallery()` so Create-a-Style updates the inline gallery without a full ribbon rebuild.

8) APPLY STYLES box (new D.applyStyles, Ctrl+Shift+S):
   - Compact WC.dialog: text input + <datalist> of WC.Styles.all(), Reapply/Modify buttons. Enter or Reapply ŌåÆ WC.applyNamedStyle(input.value) if it exists else toast. Wire Ctrl+Shift+S in app.js keymap (next to the existing Ctrl+Shift+N for Normal).

9) ROUND-TRIP / PERSISTENCE: custom-style class names live in the document HTML (E().getHTML keeps them). To make a saved doc reopen with its styles, persist `WC.Styles.custom` alongside the HTML ŌĆö extend the save payload in files.js (the .docx/.json wrapper) with a `styles` array, and on load call WC.Styles.load(arr)+injectCSS(). If file format can't carry it this pass, document that custom styles persist within a session and embed an inline `<style>` block at the top of getHTML output as a fallback so a re-opened .html keeps them (feasible, low risk).

10) CSS (ribbon.css / dialogs.css): add `.styles-list.preview .sl-item .sl-name` (no override of inline style), `.sl-glyph` styling, `.sl-item.active` highlight (reuse --word-blue-tint), `.sl-item .sl-menu` chevron (show on hover), `.style-create-preview` box, `.apply-styles-box` compact layout. Reuse existing `.style-cell` / `.styles-grid` for gallery custom cells.

specs.feature: Home-tab "extra" groups: Sensitivity (label dropdown), Editor (proofing/Editor pane), Add-ins (Get/My Add-ins), Reuse Files. All four are declared in ribbon-data.js (cmds: sensitivity, editor, addIns, reuseFiles) with feasible:"no" and currently fall through to WC.notImplemented because none have a handler in commands.js H{}.
specs.wordBehavior: SENSITIVITY: A split/dropdown showing org-defined Microsoft Purview labels (Public, General, Confidential, Highly Confidential, and sub-labels). Picking one stamps a label on the document that persists in the .docx, shows in the title bar / a sensitivity bar, can apply encryption + content marking (header/footer/watermark) and may enforce policy. The available labels come entirely from a tenant/Purview policy ŌĆö there is no built-in fixed list. EDITOR: F7 opens the right-docked Editor task pane. M365 shows an Editor "score", correction counts grouped by category (Spelling, Grammar, Refinements: Clarity/Conciseness/Formality/etc.), and lets you step through each issue with suggestion cards (accept/ignore). Classic Word 2021 instead opens the Spelling & Grammar dialog. Underlying engine is a cloud/local proofing service. ADD-INS: Dropdown with "Get Add-ins" (opens the Office Store / AppSource dialog to browse and install web add-ins) and "My Add-ins" (lists installed add-ins, Recently Used; each add-in is a sandboxed web app using Office.js that can read/modify the document and show task panes/content). REUSE FILES: A right task pane (M365 + connected account only) that searches your OneDrive/SharePoint files and lets you insert content/sections from them into the current document. Pure cloud feature.
subOptions: Sensitivity: label list Public / General / Confidential / Highly Confidential, plus a 'Remove label' entry and a footer note that labels are organizational (Purview)

Editor: top summary (Editor score + total issue count), category rows (Spelling, Grammar, Clarity/Refinements), a 'Check spelling as you type' toggle bound to the contenteditable spellcheck attribute, and per-misspelling cards with native suggestions
Add-ins: Get Add-ins (opens explanatory dialog), My Add-ins (empty-state list), Recently Used Add-ins
Reuse Files: single button/pane showing a cloud-only explanation and a disabled search box
apis: window.getSelection / Range / TreeWalker for word extraction
(optional) Electron webFrame.getWordSuggestions / contextmenu spellcheck suggestions ŌĆö falls back to a small embedded common-misspelling map if unavailable
WC.dialog, WC.flyout, WC.flyItem, WC.toast, WC.colorPalette (not needed), WC.icon
specs.uiNeeded:
FOUR distinct surfaces, all built from existing helpers:

1) SENSITIVITY ŌĆö a WC.flyout dropdown anchored on the ribbon button (cmd 'sensitivity'). Contents (WC.flyItem each): a header "Sensitivity"; the four labels Public / General / Confidential / Highly Confidential, each with a small colored swatch (green/blue/orange/red) and a check on the currently applied one; a WC.flySep; "Remove label"; then a non-clickable footer note "Labels are defined by your organization (Microsoft Purview)." Selecting a label persists it and renders a thin sensitivity bar under the ribbon plus a status-bar segment.

2) EDITOR ŌĆö a right-docked task pane (div.taskpane.right#editor-pane, mirroring D.stylesPane/D.navPane). Header "Editor" + close X. Body: (a) a summary card showing a computed "score" (100 minus weighted issues) and total issue count; (b) category rows "Spelling", "Grammar", "Refinements (Clarity & Conciseness)" each with a count badge ŌĆö Spelling is real, Grammar/Refinements show "Not available in this clone"; (c) a labeled checkbox "Check spelling as you type" wired to the editor's spellcheck attribute; (d) a scrollable list of misspelled-word cards (the flagged word, up to 3 suggestions as buttons, and "Ignore"). Clicking a suggestion replaces that occurrence; the pane re-scans.

3) ADD-INS ŌĆö a WC.flyout with WC.flyItem "Get Add-ins" and "My Add-ins". "Get Add-ins" opens a WC.dialog explaining Office Add-ins are sandboxed Office.js web apps requiring AppSource/account, with an OK button. "My Add-ins" opens a WC.dialog with an empty-state list ("No add-ins installed") and a "Recently Used Add-ins" empty section. Honest placeholder ŌĆö no runtime.

4) REUSE FILES ŌĆö clicking the button opens a WC.dialog (or small right pane) titled "Reuse Files" with a disabled search input and copy: "Reuse Files finds and inserts content from your OneDrive/SharePoint documents. It requires a Microsoft 365 work or school account and cloud storage, which this offline clone does not provide." Single Close button.
specs.implementationPlan:
All handlers go in commands.js H{} (they currently fall through to WC.notImplemented). Pane builders go in dialogs.js (D.*). No new globals required. SENSITIVITY (real, persistent): - Add H.sensitivity = (c,node) => sensitivityMenu(node). sensitivityMenu builds a WC.flyout with the four labels (color map: Public #107C10, General #0078D4, Confidential #D83B01, Highly Confidential #A4262C), "Remove label", and the org-note footer. - Store state on WC.Files.sensitivity (string|null). On pick: set it, call WC.Files.updateTitle() augmentation, render bar. - renderSensitivityBar(): create/update
inserted right under the ribbon container (or as the top child of #workarea) with the label text + swatch; remove it when label is null. Add a status-bar segment in statusbar.js init (a span updated via a tiny StatusBar.setSensitivity(label)). - PERSISTENCE: the label must round-trip through save/open. Cleanest in this codebase: have WC.Editor.getHTML() prepend (and setHTML() read+strip) a marker comment/meta, OR store as a data attribute. Concrete approach: in Files.save/saveAs payload is just html, so embed an invisible marker element at the very top of #editor:

. Editor.getHTML already clones; keep the marker in output. On setHTML, after assignment scan for .wc-sensitivity, lift data-label into WC.Files.sensitivity, call renderSensitivityBar(), and remove the node from the visible DOM is optional (hidden keeps it invisible). This makes the tag survive .docx/.html save and reopen without touching main.js. - Add a CSS rule for #sensitivity-bar in dialogs.css or base.css (thin 22px bar, left swatch, label, muted note). EDITOR PANE (real spellcheck summary + toggle): - Add H.editor = () => WC.Dialogs.editorPane(); also map the existing F7 shortcut / Review 'editor' cmd to the same so both entry points share it. Reuse the existing runSpellCheck logic conceptually but upgrade to a pane. - D.editorPane(): toggle pattern identical to D.stylesPane ŌĆö if #editor-pane exists, remove() and return; else build div.taskpane.right#editor-pane with tp-head/tp-body. - Spell scan: walk E().node text nodes with TreeWalker (skip .pagebreak-guide/.find-hit/.wc-sensitivity), tokenize words via /[A-Za-z']+/g. For each unique token, get suggestions: try Electron spellcheck if exposed (window.wordAPI.spellSuggest(word) added to preload calling webFrame; if not present) fall back to a flagging heuristic: a built-in Set of ~400 common English words is too small, so instead rely on the OS spellchecker that already underlines via spellcheck=true and flag words by checking against a bundled common-misspellings map (e.g. {teh:'the', recieve:'receive', ...}) for the suggestion cards. Honest scope: the card list is driven by the misspellings map + any words the app can confirm; the pane header states "Spelling check uses the built-in dictionary; grammar & refinements are not available." - Score: score = Math.max(0, 100 - issues*5); issues = spelling matches found. - Toggle: a checkbox bound to E().node spellcheck attribute (reuse toggle from runSpellCheck); checked reflects current attribute. - Fix action: clicking a suggestion runs a scoped find-and-replace for that exact word (reuse the TreeWalker replace approach from D.findPane's doReplace, replacing the specific text node occurrence), then re-runs the scan; "Ignore" pushes the word to an ignore Set for the session. ADD-INS (document-only placeholder, honest): - H.addIns = (c,node) => addInsMenu(node). WC.flyout with WC.flyItem('Get Add-ins', {onClick: getAddInsDialog}) and WC.flyItem('My Add-ins', {onClick: myAddInsDialog}). - getAddInsDialog: WC.dialog explaining add-ins are Office.js sandboxed web apps needing AppSource + account; footer [Close]. - myAddInsDialog: WC.dialog with empty list + "Recently Used Add-ins (none)"; footer [Close]. REUSE FILES (document-only placeholder, honest): - H.reuseFiles = () => WC.Dialogs.reuseFiles(). D.reuseFiles: WC.dialog (width 420px) with a disabled tp-search input and the cloud-only explanation, footer [Close]. Optionally render as a right pane for fidelity, but a dialog is simpler and clearly communicates it is non-functional offline. Wiring note: because dispatch in Commands.run already routes any cmd with an H[cmd] handler, simply defining these four H.* entries removes them from the notImplemented fallthrough.
